step_rationale: Lipid core of retained LDL; marker of plaque lipid burden and stability.
mechanism: Cholesteryl esters accumulate in atherosclerotic plaques as the core lipid component, marking lipid-rich plaque burden and cardiovascular risk.

references.title: Spatial lipidomics of coronary atherosclerotic plaque development in a familial hypercholesterolemia swine model.
Lipids associated with atherosclerotic plaque instability revealed by mass spectrometry imaging of human carotid arteries.
Deep Lipidomics in Human Plasma: Cardiometabolic Disease Risk and Effect of Dietary Fat Modulation.
Lipidomics Approach in High-Fat-Diet-Induced Atherosclerosis Dyslipidemia Hamsters: Alleviation Using Ether-Phospholipids in Sea Urchin.
references.journal: Journal of agricultural and food chemistry

trials.title: Investigation of Relationship Between Cholesteryl Ester Transfer Protein Deficiency and Carotid/Coronary Atherosclerosis in Patients With Hyperalphalipoproteinemia
